title: SENIOR SERVICES - 211info
text: ASHLAND SENIOR SERVICES DIVISION AND ASHLAND SENIOR CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral services and application assistance for Ashland senior utility and internet discounts and other public benefits. Offers RVTD bus and Valley Lift passes, caregiver resources, a library, a small food pantry, and assistance accessing food resources. Onsite volunteers provide help with advance directives and tutoring for computers, phones, and other devices. Offers exercise classes, social activities, special events, and a newsletter. 1699 Homes Avenue Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Age 50 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (541) 488-5342 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 488-5342 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website for more information FEES: None for most services. Exercise classes may charge a small fee. DOCUMENTS: Bus passes: photo ID and proof of address AREA SERVED: Jackson County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Next to Daniel Meyer Pool, behind Walker Elementary School. RVTD routes 10 or 17, stop at Ashland & Walker (3 blocks away). Services: Bus Fare * Older Adults Certificates/Forms Assistance * Older Adults Exercise Classes/Groups * Older Adults Food Pantries * Older Adults Special Libraries * Older Adults Specialized Information and Referral * Older Adults If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ASHLAND SENIOR SERVICES DIVISION AND ASHLAND SENIOR CENTER
